title: festuca psammophila subsp. dominii
intro: Festuca psammophila subsp. dominii, also known as Domin's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. Domin's fescue has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Festuca psammophila subsp. dominii typically involve infusions of the leaves or flowers, which can be used as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
